zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Grace Hamerton於1405年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾格林 哈莫頓。她與James De Standish I結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1468年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾霍利享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hamerton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Hammerton
meanings: English: habitational name, usually from Hammerton Green (Yorkshire) and occasionally perhaps from Hamerton (Huntingdonshire), each of which probably derives from Old English hamor, denoting some sort of plant such as hammer-wort, + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

nameMeanings.name: Grace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the abstract noun (via Old French, from Latin gratia), this name occurs occasionally in the 15th century, and by the 1540s was among the most popular girls' names in some parishes. It has always been particularly popular in Scotland and northern England (borne, for example, by Grace Darling , the lighthouse keeper's daughter whose heroism in 1838 , saving sailors in a storm, caught the popular imagination). In more recent times it was famous as the name of the actress Grace Kelly ( 1928–82 ), who became Princess Grace of Monaco. In Ireland it is used as an Anglicized form of Gráinne .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Grace Hamerton was born in 1405, in Green Hammerton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married James De Standish I. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1468, in Chorley, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Grace Hamerton(은)는 1405년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 그린 해머턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 James De Standish I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1468년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 초를리에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.
